The source instruction 'INC, 1 SC, INC' implies two increases and one single crochet, totaling three actions. The atomic actions list five actions: two increases, one sc, then two more increases. This changes the stitch pattern order and introduces extra increases not specified in the source. The total stitch count matches (5), but the sequence is wrong.

The source instruction 'INC, - SC, INC (7)' likely means: increase in first stitch, single crochet in the next 3 stitches, increase in the last stitch, for a total of 7 stitches. The atomic actions produce 9 stitches (two increases, then five single crochets, then two more increases), which changes the stitch count and stitch placement order. The warning indicates a target stitch count mismatch.
